title: Tracing Garbage Collection and Conservative Limits · Object-Lifetime Lens: Mark Sweep Invariant
summary: A language runtime must reclaim cyclic object graphs while preserving roots held by stacks, globals, and foreign code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem mark sweep invariant increment in small mark-and-sweep collector model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.
Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Mark Sweep Invariant · Theory Mark Sweep Invariant
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 4828. Produce bounded C evidence to mark reachable objects and count only unreachable sweep candidates; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Mark reachable objects, sweep only unmarked allocations, and reset metadata without losing new or mutated edges.
